reviewFinding: v3.5 created accountable advisory actions, but it still did not provide a builder-ready Decidim configuration map for spaces, components, authorizations, fake seeds, environment controls, and acceptance tests.
buildRule: Use native Decidim spaces, components, authorizations, admin roles, scheduled tasks, and production checklist controls before custom code. Custom modules are later-stage only after the fake-data blueprint passes acceptance.
advisoryRule: Dean Foley / Barayamal can review this blueprint as initial developer and trusted advisor, but cannot approve First Nations authority, privacy, Indigenous Data Sovereignty, official Toastmasters records, or private participant data access.
approvalRule: Do not build a real private Decidim pilot from this public blueprint until authority, IDS/data governance, privacy, official Toastmasters boundary, private environment, access, support, monitoring, and closeout decisions are approved privately.
safetyRule: This v3.6 blueprint is public fake/sample configuration only. It must not include real participants, member numbers, contact details, access needs, official Toastmasters records, private authority records, signatures, payments, incident details, or private pilot evidence.
noGoTriggers: A builder treats the public blueprint as authority to open real intake.
Real participant, member, official Toastmasters, access, payment, incident, or authority data appears in the public Decidim sandbox, repository, screenshots, CSVs, or JSON.
A custom Decidim module is proposed before native spaces, components, authorizations, and permissions are tested.
Dean or Barayamal is given unrestricted private records because of the advisory/developer role.
The private pilot build starts without a target Decidim version, environment owner, backup/restore proof, SMTP/job setup, admin revoke test, and redaction QA.
Official Toastmasters records, forms, payments, member numbers, or charter acceptance are mixed into the public governance commons demo.

spaces.name: System organisation shell
spaces.purpose: Create one fake First Nations Governance Commons organisation with public demo hostname only.

spaces.name: Commons public assembly
spaces.purpose: Reusable public templates, release notes, contributor rules, and non-sensitive process models.

spaces.name: Community Governance Hub
spaces.purpose: Main First Nations organisation hub with meetings, proposals, rules, accountability, and updates.

spaces.name: First Nations Toastmasters controlled sample
spaces.purpose: Fake/sample pilot process for speeches, roles, meeting feedback, motions, and learning outcomes.

spaces.name: Authority approval process
spaces.purpose: Private-only approval flow for quorum, conditions, go/hold/no-go, expiry, and stop rules.

spaces.name: IDS and privacy review process
spaces.purpose: Private-only review of data purpose, access, retention, deletion, notice, consent, and incident route.

spaces.name: Provider handoff room
spaces.purpose: Fake-data provider scope, contract gates, delivery evidence, and source-code handover.

spaces.name: Partner accountability tracker
spaces.purpose: Partner commitments, responses, due dates, public-safe progress, and unresolved issues.

spaces.name: Participatory budget rehearsal
spaces.purpose: Fake priority budget votes and allocations after proposal and meeting flows are stable.

spaces.name: Yarning circle meeting calendar
spaces.purpose: Public-safe meeting schedule, agenda, minutes template, and offline-import route.

spaces.name: Support and access route
spaces.purpose: Private support route for access needs, incidents, pause triggers, and affected-person support.

spaces.name: Public accountability updates
spaces.purpose: Public-safe what changed updates, accepted actions, delivery status, and archive summaries.

components.configuration: Plain-language process scope, who can participate, decision method, privacy boundary, and public-data rule.
components.acceptance: Page renders with fake text only.

components.configuration: Collect fake priorities and allow admin answers, states, tags, and meeting-origin links.
components.acceptance: Create, moderate, answer, and export fake proposals.

components.configuration: Publish fake agendas, registrations if needed, minutes, attachments boundary, and proposal links.
components.acceptance: Meeting minutes publish without private attendee records.

components.configuration: Fake feedback questions for confidence, facilitation, access, safety, and process clarity.
components.acceptance: Survey works with sample responses only.

components.configuration: Release notes, learning summaries, public-safe status updates, and no-authority caveats.
Accepted priorities become trackable actions with owner type, status, due date, and public evidence.
components.acceptance: Accepted fake proposal links to accountability item.

components.configuration: Fake budget projects and vote allocation after authority and proposal settings pass.
components.acceptance: Budget flow held until process rules approved.

components.configuration: Choose public-safe fake verification and private eligibility route without uploading real ID documents.
components.acceptance: No real identity documents collected in public demo.

components.configuration: Require selected authorizations for create, endorse, vote, comment, and admin actions.
components.acceptance: Unauthorized fake user cannot perform restricted action.

components.configuration: Invite sample admins, participant manager, and revoke access after test.
components.acceptance: Revoked user loses admin panel access.

components.configuration: Convert offline yarning circle outcomes into fake proposals and minutes without personal details.
components.acceptance: Offline note creates public-safe proposal summary.

components.configuration: Terms, report route, moderation reasons, cultural safety notes, and abuse response.
components.acceptance: Reported fake content can be hidden and logged.

components.configuration: State official forms, payments, member numbers, and charter acceptance stay outside the demo unless separately approved.
components.acceptance: Boundary page is visible before sample process links.

components.configuration: Use private process only for quorum and motions; public page shows status but no decision evidence.
Allow structured fake partner responses and unresolved issue notes after moderation rules pass.
components.acceptance: Partner cannot control process or access private data.

components.configuration: Send only public-safe updates after privacy notice, consent, unsubscribe, and sender identity pass.
components.acceptance: Held until SMTP and notice are approved.

components.configuration: Disable or minimise location precision unless private approval permits location fields.
components.acceptance: No precise private addresses in public content.

components.configuration: Use scheduled exports only after redaction, privacy, and data-governance review.
components.acceptance: Public export excludes private and official records.

permissionRules.allowed: View public-safe pages, proposals, meetings, updates, release notes, and fake sample data.
permissionRules.limit: Cannot create, vote, see private evidence, or infer authority approval.

permissionRules.allowed: Comment or propose only where the process allows it and public terms are accepted.
permissionRules.limit: No real pilot participation until private approval.

permissionRules.allowed: Can support, vote, or submit in restricted components after approved fake/private authorization.
Manage assigned spaces, components, proposals, meetings, pages, and accountability items.
permissionRules.limit: Cannot approve authority, privacy, IDS, or official records alone.

permissionRules.allowed: Chairs private motions, conditions, expiry, go/hold/no-go, and stop-rule decisions.
Advises on process legitimacy, cultural safety, and meeting protocol where invited.
permissionRules.limit: Cultural material never public by default.

permissionRules.allowed: Creates meeting summaries and offline import records using approved public-safe templates.
permissionRules.limit: No private attendance or access-needs details in public notes.

permissionRules.allowed: Reviews public package, asks questions, raises findings, and checks build feasibility.
permissionRules.limit: Cannot approve pilot or receive unrestricted private evidence.

permissionRules.allowed: Builds, configures, tests, and documents fake-data environment as initial developer/custodian.
permissionRules.limit: Developer custody is not First Nations authority.

permissionRules.allowed: Reviews public build pack, fake seed data, and acceptance tests for quoting or delivery.
permissionRules.limit: No production export or private data.

permissionRules.allowed: Keeps official forms, member numbers, payments, signatures, and charter pathway outside demo.
permissionRules.limit: Official records not stored in Decidim unless separately approved.

permissionRules.allowed: Approves data classes, access, retention, deletion, reporting, export, and return route.
Approves notice, consent, withdrawal, correction, minimisation, and incident response.
permissionRules.limit: No real intake until privacy decision passes.

permissionRules.allowed: Approves pause trigger, support route, incident handling, and affected-person support.
Handles impersonation/support workflows if enabled for admin assistance.
permissionRules.limit: Use only after privacy and access approval.
No admin, evidence, exports, messages, or participant access.

Private status only; public shows owner type and hold.
Private motion shell only; public package records no decision evidence.
Private-only data-governance record placeholder.
Private-only privacy record placeholder.
Boundary note for forms, member numbers, payments, signatures, and charter acceptance.
Fake-data scope, no production export, and quote assumptions.

environmentControls.proof: Choose supported version and document Ruby, Node, database, and upgrade path before build.
Use Decidim app generation flow rather than cloning core source for a normal installation.
Create database role and separate development, staging, and production databases.
Confirm frontend assets build and precompile in the chosen environment.
Confirm ImageMagick/LibVips and ActiveStorage decisions before attachments.
Do not send real notifications until sender identity, notice, and unsubscribe are approved.
Configure mail digest, reminders, open-data export, inactive participant cleanup, and other required tasks.
Create system admin, create organisation, then invite organisation admin through controlled route.
Test least privilege, admin revocation, participant manager route, and audit evidence privately.
Run backup and restore evidence privately before real pilot data exists.
Keep public fake demo, private staging, and production data isolated.
Name uptime, errors, dependency updates, security patches, and incident contact.
Disable precise location features unless privacy and data reviewers approve.
Hold open data exports until redaction and IDS/privacy review pass.

acceptanceTests.expected: Visitor can open fake organisation without framework errors.
Admin creates sample proposal, changes state, and publishes public-safe answer.
Admin publishes fake agenda and minutes without attendee details.
Facilitator imports public-safe offline outcome into proposal or meeting summary.
Unverified fake user cannot perform restricted support/vote action.
Sample admin loses access after revoke test.
Dean reviewer can see public package only, not private vault or admin panels.
Provider reviewer can access fake build pack but not private records or exports.
Official forms, payments, member numbers, signatures, and charter acceptance stay outside demo.
Budget component remains held until proposal and authority rules pass.
Accepted fake proposal creates or links to accountability item.
Core flow renders on 390 px viewport and avoids oversized media.
Private restore evidence is recorded before real data collection.
Repository, CSVs, JSON, screenshots, and release notes contain fake/sample data only.
Fake incident tabletop pauses process, routes support, and blocks public details.
Public package shows status only; private decision evidence stays outside demo.
